frozen_components: steering vectors (computed offline from contrastive hidden-state differences)
architecture.summary: ATLAS is a test-time adaptive steering framework for LLMs that combines latent process verification with activation steering. Offline, it extracts contrastive steering vectors for execution, reflection, and transition modes by comparing hidden states at thought boundaries, and trains a lightweight MLP verifier on PRM-supervised step-quality scores. During inference, at each thought boundary, the verifier scores candidate intervened hidden states induced by four discrete actions (no intervention, execution, reflection, transition), and selects the action with highest predicted quality before generation continues. Information flows from hidden states → verifier → action selection → intervention application → next generation step.
trained_components: latent verifier (lightweight MLP with two hidden layers)

biblio.title: ATLAS: Verifier-Guided Adaptive Latent Activation Steering for Efficient LLM Reasoning

claims.statement: On R1-Distill-1.5B, ATLAS improves MATH accuracy from 73.76% to 82.28% while reducing token usage by 30.1%
On R1-Distill-1.5B, ATLAS improves GSM8K accuracy from 79.30% to 85.37% while reducing token usage by 44.9%
On R1-Distill-1.5B in cross-domain transfer, ATLAS improves AIME2024 accuracy from 20.00% to 33.33% while reducing token usage by 14.8%
On R1-Distill-1.5B in cross-domain transfer, ATLAS improves AMC2023 accuracy from 47.50% to 65.00% with a 26.8% token reduction

ATLAS latent verifier achieves comparable accuracy to text-level PRM verification (ATLAS-T) on in-domain benchmarks while using fewer generated tokens, e.g., 83.83% accuracy with 2035 tokens vs ATLAS-T 83.75% accuracy with 2118 tokens on R1-Distill-1.5B

Smaller models (R1-Distill-1.5B/7B) select reflection and transition actions more frequently, while larger models rely more heavily on execution, suggesting that weaker models benefit more from corrective and exploratory interventions
Latent verifier prediction remains robust under Gaussian perturbations, with absolute prediction change staying insignificant (0.01) for noise scales up to 0.25σ

ATLAS provides a unified framework for combining learned latent verification with test-time activation steering, enabling adaptive reasoning control without additional LLM decoding or inference-time process reward model calls
